decks.notes: This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy while maintaining a fast, aggressive tempo. Utilizing cards like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Daze to control the board and counter threats, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf. By efficiently managing resources and applying pressure early on, this Dimir aggro deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent to secure a swift victory.

The main strategy of this deck is to establish a lock on the game by assembling a combination of artifacts that restrict the opponent's options and resources. By using cards like Trinisphere, Karn, the Great Creator, and Planar Nexus, the deck aims to control the board and prevent the opponent from executing their game plan effectively. The deck also utilizes powerful artifacts like Urza's Tower, Grim Monolith, and Ugin, Eye of the Storms to generate mana and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Ultimately, the goal is to outvalue the opponent and win through resource denial and powerful artifact synergies.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures like Amped Raptor and Ocelot Pride, supported by powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Static Prison to control the opponent's plays. With efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares and a resilient manabase with fetch lands and dual lands, this deck aims to apply consistent pressure on the opponent and close out the game before they can establish their own game plan.

This deck focuses on using countermagic, removal spells, and land disruption to control the game while generating card advantage through card draw engines like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Quantum Riddler. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan by denying key spells with Force of Will and Stifle, as well as by destroying their lands with Wasteland. The creatures in the deck provide additional threats and card advantage, allowing the pilot to outvalue the opponent in the long game and secure victory through incremental advantage.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly establish board presence and apply pressure to the opponent through efficient creatures and removal spells. The deck utilizes cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Goblin Bombardment to disrupt the opponent's game plan while also developing its own board. The inclusion of cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls provides additional value and synergy with the aggressive strategy. By leveraging a combination of creature abilities, removal, and efficient mana base, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through combat damage.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively deploy creatures such as Amped Raptor and Guide of Souls to overwhelm the opponent and deal damage quickly. Utilizing cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah, the deck aims to control the board and remove opposing threats efficiently. Additionally, cards like Swords to Plowshares and Cabal Therapy help in disrupting the opponent's game plan while cards like Wasteland and Thoughtseize provide further disruption and control. With a mix of aggressive creatures and disruption spells, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent in order to secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, then using spells like Reanimate and Shallow Grave to bring them back into play for a devastating combo. Cards like Collective Brutality and Cabal Therapy help disrupt the opponent's strategy while Thoughtseize and Unmask provide additional disruption and hand control. The deck also utilizes mana acceleration with cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual to ensure a quick and explosive reanimation strategy. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disruption spells.

This deck revolves around utilizing artifacts to create a prison-like control strategy. By combining powerful artifacts like Karn, the Great Creator, Urza's Saga, and Grim Monolith with cards like Trinisphere and Pithing Needle, the deck aims to lock down the opponent's options and prevent them from executing their game plan effectively. The inclusion of big threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms provides a win condition to close out the game once the opponent is locked down.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying powerful artifacts such as The One Ring and Karn, the Great Creator to overwhelm opponents with aggressive plays. utilizing cards like Thundertrap Trainer and Stock Up to maintain tempo and card advantage. The deck also includes disruption elements like Force of Will and Pithing Needle to control the board while using Urza lands and Planar Nexus to generate mana for big plays. The overall strategy is to establish a strong board presence early on and maintain pressure throughout the game with a combination of artifacts and aggressive spells.

The main concept of this deck is to maintain control of the game by using cheap counterspells and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The goal is to deploy threats such as Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent quickly to apply pressure and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize. The inclusion of Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and the ability to filter through the deck for key spells. The deck also includes land destruction elements with Wasteland to further disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The following deck focuses on locking down the opponent's resources and controlling the game through artifacts, utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Trinisphere, and Planar Nexus. By using powerful artifacts like Urza's Tower and Candelabra of Tawnos, the deck aims to establish a prison-like board state that restricts the opponent's options. With cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn providing additional threats, the deck aims to control the game and win through a combination of resource denial and powerful finishers.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo of Aluren and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to generate value and disrupt opponents. By utilizing cards such as Show and Tell, Omniscience, and Stock Up, the deck aims to create a powerful board state while also having access to disruptive elements like Force of Will and Veil of Summer. Acererak the Archlich serves as a win condition in the deck, providing an alternate path to victory if the combo pieces are not accessible. The deck seeks to quickly and efficiently execute its game plan to overwhelm opponents and secure a win.

The main concept of this Legacy Combo Aluren deck is to quickly assemble Aluren and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to create a powerful combo that allows you to cast creatures for free. The deck also includes cards like Acererak the Archlich, Show and Tell, and Omniscience to cheat big creatures into play. With the use of cantrips like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Veil of Summer, along with disruption from cards like Force of Will, this deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with its combo potential and powerful creatures while controlling the game.

The deck revolves around assembling the Painter's Servant and Grindstone combo to mill your opponent's library in one turn. The deck has multiple ways to tutor for the combo pieces with cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Urza's Saga, and Mishra's Research Desk. It also includes key artifacts like Voltaic Key and Manifold Key to untap the Grindstone for multiple activations in a single turn. The deck also features disruption elements like Pithing Needle and Soul-Guide Lantern to control the game and protect its combo.

The deck focuses on using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive spells to control the game and disrupt the opponent's plans. It aims to establish control of the board through counterspells like Force of Will and disruptive spells like Stifle, while also utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo and Teferi to generate card advantage and outvalue the opponent. The deck also includes efficient removal options like Swords to Plowshares to deal with threats. Overall, the deck is built to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent until it can secure victory.

The deck aims to quickly and consistently take advantage of high-risk, high-reward combo plays by utilizing a combination of cheap spells, ritual effects, and card draw to rapidly cycle through the deck and win the game in one explosive turn. It looks to aggressively mill itself, fueling graveyard interactions like Dread Return or Reanimate, while carefully managing resources to protect the game-winning combo with Pact of Negation. By operating on the edge of chaos, the deck requires precise timing and decision-making to pull off its game-ending combos and overwhelm the opponent before they can respond.
